steps.title: Fit the table as an exponential
steps.content: Enter the values in Desmos: $x_1=[-1,0,1,2]$ and $y_1=[\frac{1}{69},1,69,4761]$. Then run $y_1\sim ab^{x_1}$. Desmos returns $a=1$ and $b=69$, so the values fit $y=69^x$.

steps.title: Determine direction of exponential function
steps.content: The same pattern is clear by ratios: $1\div \frac{1}{69}=69$, $69\div1=69$, and $4761\div69=69$. The constant ratio means exponential, and because $69>1$, the function is increasing. That matches choice C.
